Episode 331: Engage in Networking Education (Get Connected–Stay Connected, Part 2)

November 6, 2013 by Ivan Misner 5 Comments

Podcast: Download

Subscribe: Apple Podcasts | RSS | Subscription Options

Print This Episode
This entry is in the series Get Connected Stay Connected

Synopsis

BNI Great Lakes District Director Deanna Tucci-Schmitt joins Dr. Misner this week for the second episode in the “Get Connected–Stay Connected” series. This episode focuses on how to use BNI Connect for networking education.

Confucius said “I came to learn what I already know,” and this is especially appropriate for BNI training. Available education units are all listed on BNI Connect. You can find podcasts, newsletters, and webinars on BNI Connect. To access the webinars, log on to BNI Connect and click on the circle with the question mark in it. For every 5 podcasts or webinars you listen to, you get a point toward your CEUs, which you can track on BNI Connect. Submit your CEU slips on your “my business” page.

Be sure to join Dr. Misner’s “From the Founder” group on BNI Connect.

Brought to you by Networking Now.

Complete Transcript of BNI Podcast Episode 331 –

Priscilla:
Hello everyone and welcome back to The Official BNI Podcast brought to you by NetworkingNow.com, which is the leading site on the net for networking downloadables. I am Priscilla Rice, and I am coming to you from Live Oak Recording Studio in Berkeley, CA. I am joined on the phone today by the Founder and Chairman of BNI, Dr. Ivan Misner. Hello Ivan. How are you?

Ivan:
I am doing great, Priscilla. This week we are at the International Conference for BNI. Usually this week of every year and today’s topic is the second in the series “Get Connected Stay Connected”. This one is Get Connected Stay Connected: Engage in Networking Education.

I have a special guest who is with me today. This is her third appearance on the BNI Podcast. She is episode 160. I love the title of that: Spinach in Your Teeth. And episode 220, Culture of Recruiting. We have with us today Deanna Tucci-Schmitt. Deanna brought BNI in 1998 to the western Pennsylvania area. Her and her team of directors provide structure, training and coaching to over 700 business owners and sales people in BNI. She is also a BNI District Director. That is a big deal, for those of you who don’t know. She is a BNI Disctrict Director for Ohio, Minnesota, Michigan, Milwaukee and Indiana. And she is a contributing author to the bestselling books, both Masters of Networking and Masters of Sales. But most importantly- well, I can’t say that. Importantly, Deanna is also coming from Pittsburgh, PA, my hometown. Deanna, welcome.

Deanna:
Thank you. And that is my most important link.

Ivan:
I don’t buy it but I appreciate.

Deanna:
I am glad to be here. I am glad to be here. Engage in Networking Education.

Ivan:
And there are three things that we would like to hit on in this podcast. First of all BNI Connect is an amazing tool for members. That is what we want to talk about. We are doing this year-long campaign on getting on BNI Connect and staying connected. When it comes to education, Deanna, there are three things that I want to talk about today.

The first thing is how members can benefit from networking education and how it relates to BNI Connect.

Deanna:
Networking education is always, always good. We are going to talk about it as it pertains to the continuing education units, the CEUs, that can be tracked on BNI Connect. One of my favorite things is I came to learn what I already know. I came to learn what I already know. Confucius said that, particularly a great saying when it comes to education on your networking skills because it is an ongoing process and I swear every time I listen to the orientation CD, I pick up something new or get refreshed on something new. So I came to learn what I already know.

Let’s talk about how it benefits members to engage in networking education as it pertains to the CEUs, the chapter education units. I bet you a lot of members didn’t know that they can actually track their education units right on BNI Connect on My BNI Business page.

Ivan:
I think you are right. A lot of people probably don’t know that, so tell us a little bit about it.

Deanna
Well, there are many different ways to get education in BNI. That is one of the things I think that sets up apart from other business referral organizations, other networking organizations. We have so many resources and these resources help member to be able to help teach their fellow members, their sales force, how to find referrals for them more effectively.

Any little bit of self education that you do- you know, I think it is Harvey McKay who says, “Sharpen the saw.” It’s something you always want to be doing. Yes, you should be sharpening the saw of your business, but also of your networking skills because you are teaching your fellow members how to find you business. You can do this in as little as 10 minutes on podcasts like this, or you can read books, but when you look at BNI Connect, there is a listing.

I will tell you how to access all this. But you are wondering, gee, what can I do to capture some education units. Well, they are all listed right there on BNI Connect. There are the CDs. There’s the SuccessNet. We can talk about some of these. The online free newsletter that we have that is just packed full of information.

Ivan:
It has a decade’s worth of content up there that people can draw down from.

Deanna:
A decade’s worth. That is remarkable. There are podcasts- these podcasts are my particular favorite. I have to tell you a story about the podcast. We have a director in BNI. He is a member in BNI. He lives in one location and he drives 50 miles to his chapter meeting once a week, every Wednesday. He has been a member for five years. He has only missed one meeting. He first learned about podcasts. He listened to every single podcast in his commute back and forth. He was so energized and so intelligent about how to market his business in BNI.

We have those podcasts. We have webinars. A great story about this. The BNI webinars came about when we added BNI Connect. Correct? Isn’t that when we added BNI webinars?

Ivan:
That is correct. We started doing webinars with BNI Connect, as a way of being able to teach people globally.

Deanna:
You do them and Jeremy does them. Jeremy says there are a ton of them out there. One of the chapters as an assignment, says each week a different member is going to go to BNI Connect and pick a webinar of your choice and then come back the next day and summarize it in three minutes. Every single person said that oh my gosh, there is so much great information there. They just couldn’t believe it. They were delighted.

I love that feedback about all the opportunities there. Again, we can tell you later on in the podcast or at the end of the podcast, how to access those webinars.

Ivan:
How about now? Can you tell them how to access it?

Deanna:
Absolutely. Sure. You log on to BNI Connect by inputting your user name and password. And then there is a little circle on the right side of the page with a question mark on it. If you click on there, there it is, the current podcasts are listed right there and all the prior ones are below that. There are, as Jeremy said, a ton of them- whatever you want to talk about like how to develop social media for your profile. You name it it’s there.

Ivan:
There is an opportunity to ask questions on those webinars, and of course, you can see all the past ones prior to this November recording. You can also see what is coming up as well. There is just so much content, and you described it well. Just go to BNI Connect. Click on the question mark and that takes you to the support section, which includes all the webinars.

Deanna:
It’s all there. For everything that you do to develop your networking education, you can develop credits. You can get CEUs. So for every five podcasts and/or webinars that you listen to, you get a point. For every SuccessNet that you read, you get a point. For doing the Member Success Program, two points. Leadership Team training is another way to continue your networking education. That’s three points. We just got finished doing that back in September. You can go to that back in Advanced Trainings. Other BNI-endorsed workshops.

By the way, I am just going down the list. Other networking workshops, Referral Institute, Room Full of Referrals. If there is a Referral Institute in your area, that is a great way to develop five points. Then of course, the books by Dr. Ivan Misner. How many are there?

Ivan:
Seventeen, but who’s counting?

Deanna:
Five points for reading a book. It’s just so simple. First of all, this is literally at your fingertips. When John was listening to them on his commute, he listened to them on his smartphone. He just had to click a button, Google search and there they are. Then, after you are done doing that every week, you can just go again and log on to BNI Connect. Then you will be on My BNI Business page. Right there it says “Submit CEU Slip”. You open that up and it couldn’t be easier. You just enter the quantity of what you listened to and you hit submit and it tabulates your CEU units.

Ivan:
We are almost out of time. You hit my other two questions, which were the resources available at your fingertips. They are right there at the question mark. And you just explained how to upload continuing education credits, which I think is great. You know, a lot of the new cars have a little plug so you don;t have to listen to it on your device. You can plug it in to the speakers itself. That is what I do. You can really hear this very, very well. Any closing thoughts before we wrap up today?

Deanna:
A great story. Yesterday I was talking to a member in Gilbrest. You never know. You never know when you get connected how it is going to turn out. He told me a great story that he went on vacation to Myrtle Beach recently. He is from Pittsburgh. When he visited, he visited a chapter down there. He called a member of the chapter to let them know he was coming, to get invited. This person happened to be the promotional person for the Myrtle Beach minor league baseball team. He went to the meeting. That person gave him eight tickets because his whole family of course was on vacation and they sat right behind home plate. So I mean, it’s not a referral, but what fun would that be!

Ivan:
It would be great. It is like with BNI Connect, you can have a friend in every city. There are so many people that I have met who said they visited a region, pulled up somebody on BNI Connect and then the people just rolled out the red carpet to them. That is the kind of thing that you can do with BNI Connect.

Deanna:
Thanks for being here today. This is the second of the series, Get Connected Stay Connected. We talked about networking education. Please join me on BNI Connect. Join Deanna and myself on BNI Connect. If you get on, join my group, From the Founder. You can see a lot of the things that are going on in the organization. That I post about. You can talk to me directly on BNI Connect by going to the From the Founder group. It is one of the largest groups on BNI Connect so far. Deanna, thank you so much. We appreciate all that you do. Back to you, Priscilla.

Priscilla:
Okay. Thank you both. I appreciated that. I think that is it for this week.I would just like to remind the listeners that this podcast has been brought to you by networkingnow.com, the leading site on the net for networking downloadables. Thanks you very much for listening. This is Priscilla Rice, and we hope you will join us next week for another exciting episode of The Official BNI Podcast.

ShareTweetLinkedinWhatsappSaveSendFlipboard

Part of the series Get Connected Stay ConnectedSpecial Guest Deanna Tucci-Schmitt Posted in Education Coordinators, Online Networking Tagged with BNI Connect

Comments

    Solutions-focused comments are welcome. Dr. Misner reads every comment personally.

    All comments are moderated before posting. Profanity, personal attacks, and sales pitches will be deleted.

  1. Zurriane Bennett says

    November 8, 2013 at 7:32 am

    There is a motivational speaker by the name of Les Brown. He said that if you really want something “You have to be Hungry!!!!” Well without question I am HUNGRY!!!! I am sure that I have listened to over 200 podcasts, watched many BNI videos, attended more BNI training then many that have been in the organization for several more years then me. I have read about 100 BNI articles, read several of Dr. Misner’s books and the books of other suggested authors etc. However my region and I do not have access to BNI Connect. It saddens me that several of those who have access are not using it. Confucius said “I came to learn what I already know,” and this is especially appropriate for BNI training. Great quote! I now have an idea what many Africa Americans felt like that could not get access to education, cutting edge information and useful tools. If you have access to BNI Connect know now you have a great opportunity, use it. Best wishes and success to you all.

    Reply

  2. Sherry Carmichael says

    November 11, 2013 at 6:34 pm

    I recently became the VP in our BNI group and I have been really listening to more of the podcasts since the beginning of Oct. I love the education that I receive from these podcasts and have been suggesting to the group to do them more also. I know it will help us all grow personally and also help each other find more business for each other!! And I especially really enjoyed this podcast because I am also from the great city of Pittsburgh and just learned from this one that you are too Ivan!!! Love that great city and hope to move back someday!! Thanks so much for making all these learning tools so accessable for everyone!

    Reply

  3. Danealle Marshall (@DMTitleChikk) says

    January 11, 2016 at 2:18 am

    Is there a way when we submit our slips for the BNI Podcasts and webinars, we can enter the numbers for tracking and accountability purposes? Thank you.

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      February 3, 2016 at 10:39 am

      Hi Danealle. You should reach out to your local BNI Director. This shouldn’t be a problem. I know of regions that alloow Podcasts to be used as CEU credits for BNI.

      Reply

  4. Silvio Acherboim says

    October 8, 2025 at 5:41 am

    Permanecer conectado é com certeza um dos principais pontos do BNI.
    Mas o mais importante é fazer com que outras pessoas entendam o que você faz.
    Se as pessaos entenderem o que faz de forma simples e direta a conexão será automática e permanente.
    Gratidão pelo podcast.
    Abs
    Silvio

    Reply
